branch_thesis_statement: Accessibility Topology studies whether recoverable transformational futures remain connected, traversable, and rewovable under recursive constraint.

core_distinction.accessibility_geometry: Concerned with accessibility measure, deformation, gradients, and transformational accessibility volume.
core_distinction.accessibility_topology: Concerned with connectivity, traversability, fragmentation, rewovability, bottlenecks, and continuity structure.

transformational_accessibility_structure.name: Transformational Accessibility Structure
transformational_accessibility_structure.definition: Defines what transformational futures are survivably reachable.

transformational_reopenability_measure.name: Transformational Reopenability Measure
transformational_reopenability_measure.definition: Defines which reachable transformations preserve recoverable continuity.

recoverable_accessibility_fibers.name: Recoverable Accessibility Fibers
recoverable_accessibility_fibers.definition: Fiber structures carrying recoverable transformational continuity across recursive horizons.

accessibility_connectivity_topology.name: Accessibility Connectivity Topology
accessibility_connectivity_topology.definition: Connectivity structure governing traversability and rewovability of recoverable transformational accessibility.

core_questions: Can recoverable transformational pathways still be traversed?
Can fragmented accessibility fibers still be rewoven?
Can local viability persist despite global fragmentation?
When does topological viability fail before metric viability vanishes?

topological_collapse.interpretation: Accessible futures still exist, but they no longer form a sufficiently connected recoverable structure.

topological_precollapse.statement: Topological viability may fail before metric viability fully vanishes.

rewovability_principle.statement: Viability is the rewovability of regenerative accessibility fibers.

canonical_compressions: Accessibility Topology studies whether recoverable transformational futures remain connected, traversable, and rewovable under recursive constraint.
Topological viability may fail before metric viability fully vanishes.
Accessible futures still exist, but they no longer form a sufficiently connected recoverable structure.
Collapse occurs when regenerative accessibility fibers can no longer be rewoven.
Viability is the rewovability of regenerative accessibility fibers.
one_sentence_lock: Accessibility Topology studies whether recoverable transformational futures remain connected, traversable, and rewovable under recursive constraint.
